perturbation.original_text: The Company hereby appoints Deutsche Bank as the initial Remarketing Agent, and Deutsche 3 Bank hereby accepts appointment as Remarketing Agent, for the purpose of (i) Remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof, (ii) establishing the Reset Rates (as defined below) for the Securities in connection with the Remarketing and (iii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company hereby appoints Deutsche Bank as the initial Remarketing Entity, and Deutsche 3 Bank hereby accepts designation as Remarketing Entity, for the purpose of (i) Remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof, (ii) establishing the Reset Rates (as defined below) for the Securities in connection with the Remarketing and (iii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Remarketing Agent' is changed to 'Remarketing Entity' in this section. However, the contract continues to use 'Remarketing Agents' throughout the document, including in the same sentence, creating confusion about whether these terms are interchangeable. This contradiction affects the responsibilities and obligations outlined for the Remarketing Agents in subsequent sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Remarketing Agents agree (i) to use commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the Remarketed Securities tendered or deemed tendered to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing, (ii) to establish the Reset Rates in accordance with the Remarketing Procedures and to notify the Company, the Trustee and the Stock Purchase Contract Agent promptly of the Reset Rates and (iii) to carry out such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with the provisions of the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Remarketing Agent' to 'Remarketing Entity' introduces a contradiction because the contract continues to use 'Remarketing Agents' in the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states '(b) The Remarketing Agents agree...' implying a specific defined role. By changing the initial definition to 'Remarketing Entity' but retaining 'Remarketing Agents' elsewhere, the contract creates ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding the responsibilities and obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' which states that 'the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: To furnish to the Remarketing Agents a copy of each proposed Issuer Free Writing Prospectus prepared by or on behalf of, used by, or referred to by the Company and not to use or refer to any proposed Issuer Free Writing Prospectus to which the Remarketing Agents reasonably object; if at any time following issuance of an Issuer Free Writing Prospectus there occurred or occurs an event or development as a result of which such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus conflicted or would conflict with the information contained in the Registration Statement, the Disclosure Package, the Final Prospectus or any Preliminary Prospectus or, when taken together with the Disclosure Package and any other such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, included or would include an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted or would omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in the light of the circumstances prevailing at that subsequent time, not misleading, to promptly notify the Remarketing Agents and, if requested by the Remarketing Agents, to promptly amend or supplement, at its own expense, such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus to eliminate or correct such conflict, untrue statement or omission; provided, however, that this covenant shall not apply to any statements or omissions in an Issuer Free Writing Prospectus made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing to the Company by any Remarketing Agent expressly for use therein;
perturbation.changed_text: To furnish to the Remarketing Agents a copy of each proposed Issuer Free Writing Prospectus prepared by or on behalf of, used by, or referred to by the Company and not to use or refer to any proposed Issuer Free Writing Prospectus to which the Remarketing Lead reasonably object; if at any time following issuance of an Issuer Free Writing Prospectus there occurred or occurs an event or development as a result of which such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus conflicted or would conflict with the information contained in the Registration Statement, the Disclosure Package, the Final Prospectus or any Preliminary Prospectus or, when taken together with the Disclosure Package and any other such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, included or would include an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted or would omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in the light of the circumstances prevailing at that subsequent time, not misleading, to promptly notify the Remarketing Agents and, if requested by the Remarketing Agents, to promptly amend or supplement, at its own expense, such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus to eliminate or correct such conflict, untrue statement or omission; provided, however, that this covenant shall not apply to any statements or omissions in an Issuer Free Writing Prospectus made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing to the Company by any Remarketing Agent expressly for use therein;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Remarketing Agents' is changed to 'Remarketing Lead' but just in the first time it appears. However, the contract continues to use 'Remarketing Agents' throughout the document, including in the same section, creating confusion about whether the objections are valid if only being made by the lead, or if there should be an agreement between all of the agents. This contradiction affects the validity of the prospectus.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) To furnish the Remarketing Agents with copies of any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus in such quantities as the Remarketing Agents may from time to time reasonably request, and if, at any time prior to the earlier of (i) the completion of the distribution of the Remarketed Securities and (ii) the expiration of nine months after the date of the Final Prospectus, any event shall have occurred as a result of which any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus as then amended or supplemented would include an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state any material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made when such Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus were delivered, not misleading, or, if for any 19 other reason it shall be necessary during such period to amend or supplement any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus or to file under the Exchange Act any document incorporated by reference in any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus in order to comply with the Act or the Exchange Act, (i) to notify the Remarketing Agents and (ii) upon their request to prepare and furnish without charge to each Remarketing Agent and to any dealer in securities as many copies as such Remarketing Agent may from time to time reasonably request of an amended Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or a supplement to the Final Prospectus or an amended Final Prospectus which will correct such statement or omission or effect such compliance; and any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus and the Final Prospectus and any amendments or supplements thereto furnished to the Remarketing Agents shall be identical to the electronically transmitted copies thereof filed with the Commission pursuant to EDGAR, except to the extent permitted by Regulation S-T;
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Remarketing Agents'. The changed text alters this to 'Remarketing Lead' in the first instance but then reverts to 'Remarketing Agents' in the same section and in the contradicted text. This creates an ambiguity as to whether the objection or request needs to come from all agents or only the lead agent. This directly contradicts the other uses of 'Remarketing Agents', as it becomes unclear who has the authority to object or request actions regarding the Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus. This misalignment, as defined in 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', leads to conflicting obligations. The changed text modifies the meaning from requiring the consent/request from all 'Remarketing Agents' to only requiring it from 'Remarketing Lead' which creates a direct contradiction with the rest of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Remarketing Agents agree that it would not be just and equitable if contributions pursuant to this subsection (d) were determined by pro rata allocation (even if the Remarketing Agents were treated as one entity for such purpose) or by any other method of allocation which does not take account of the equitable considerations referred to above in this subsection (d).
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Underwriters agree that it would not be just and equitable if contributions pursuant to this subsection (d) were determined by pro rata allocation (even if the Remarketing Agents were treated as one entity for such purpose) or by any other method of allocation which does not take account of the equitable considerations referred to above in this subsection (d).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Remarketing Agents' is changed to 'Underwriters'. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the term wasn't defined and it continues to use the term Remarketing Agents in the original text, creating confusion about who the parties referred to in this section are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All statements, requests, notices and agreements hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given if mailed or transmitted by any standard form of telecommunication; notices to the Remarketing Agents shall be directed to Deutsche Bank Securities Inc., 60 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005, with a copy to Debevoise & Plimpton LLP, 919 Third Avenue, New York, New York 10022, attention of Peter J. Loughran, Esq.; notices to the Company shall be delivered or sent by mail, telex or facsimile transmission to the address of the Company set forth in the Registration Statement, Attention: General Counsel, with a copy to Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP, 787 Seventh Avenue, New York, NY 10019, attention John M. Schwolsky, Esq. and Vladimir Nicenko, Esq.; notices to the Trustee shall be delivered or sent by mail or facsimile transmission to The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A., 2 North LaSalle Street, Suite 1020, Chicago, Illinois 60602, telecopy: (312) 827-8542; notices to the Stock Purchase Contract Agent shall be delivered or sent by mail or facsimile transmission to Deutsche Bank Trust Company Americas, Trust and Securities Services, 60 Wall Street, 16th Floor, MS: NYC60-2710, New York, NY 10005, telecopy: (732) 578-4635; and notices to the Collateral Agent or the Custodial Agent shall be delivered or sent by mail or facsimile transmission to Deutsche Bank Trust Company Americas, Trust and Securities Services, 60 Wall Street, 16th Floor, MS: NYC60-2710, New York, NY 10005, telecopy: (732) 578-4635. Any such statements, requests, notices or agreements shall take effect at the time of receipt thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Remarketing Agents,' and the contradicted text provides contact information specifically for 'Remarketing Agents.' The changed text replaces 'Remarketing Agents' with 'Underwriters' in one instance but leaves the term 'Remarketing Agents' in the original text. This creates an ambiguity because the reader will be unsure whether the term 'Remarketing Agents' used in the original text is the same as the Underwriters, and whether the contact information in the contradicted text should now apply to the 'Underwriters', potentially misaligning obligations and creating confusion about notice procedures. As defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', this is a clear contradiction.
